reasoning: The market estimate prior to the September CPI data release was 13%. The September CPI data, released on October 24, 2025, showed year-over-year inflation at 3.0%, continuing a trend around 3.0% for the year (January 2025 was around 3.0%, August was 2.9%). Since the Bureau of Labor Statistics CPI is around 3.0% with only three months remaining in the year, it is virtually mathematically impossible for the 2025 average to fall below 2.5%, and extremely unlikely that the December 2025 year-over-year figure will drop by over 0.5% in a single quarter without severe, unexpected deflation. A 3% probability recognizes the extremely narrow path to resolving YES.
